decisions.decision: Use CSS transitions + requestAnimationFrame + SVG <animate> for animations instead of adding D3 transition dependency. BayesNetGraph is intentionally D3-free and should stay lightweight.
alternatives_rejected: Add d3-transition and use D3 selections for animation

decisions.decision: Trigger animation by watching AssumptionContext's assumptions array via useEffect in BayesNetGraph, providing instant visual feedback on assumption toggle before API response.
alternatives_rejected: Hook into useProbabilityEdit onSuccess (delays animation until API returns)
Add onAssumptionChange callback to context (more invasive)

decisions.decision: Use BFS traversal from evidence node along merged edges (conditionalEdges + clique-derived edges from deriveEdgesFromCliques) for propagation order simulation.
alternatives_rejected: Junction-tree message-passing order (clique data lacks elimination ordering)

decisions.decision: Animate all BFS-reachable nodes from evidence node, not just those with changed marginals. Since animation fires before API response, new marginals aren't available for comparison.
alternatives_rejected: Compare pre/post marginals from React Query cache (timing issues, marginals not yet updated at animation start)

decisions.decision: Use 150ms stagger per BFS hop, cap total at 1.5s, make animation interruptible via AbortController/ref-stored cleanup. Evidence node highlight 0.2s, node glow 0.3s on arrival.
alternatives_rejected: Non-interruptible animation (causes visual confusion on rapid toggles)

decisions.decision: Increase SVG PADDING from 40 to 60 to accommodate glow filter effects that extend beyond node bounds.
alternatives_rejected: Add overflow='visible' to SVG (less predictable cross-browser)

steps.task: Create useAnimationPropagation hook: manages animation state (highlighted nodes set, active edges set, animation phase), performs BFS from evidence node along merged edges, schedules staggered timeouts per hop (150ms), supports cancellation via cleanup ref for interruptibility. Returns { animatingNodes, animatingEdges, triggerAnimation, cancelAnimation }.

Add SVG filter definitions (<defs>) to BayesNetGraph for glow/highlight effects: feGaussianBlur-based glow filter for evidence node, feDropShadow for arrival highlight. Add corresponding CSS classes with keyframe animations for pulse effect.
Increase PADDING constant from 40 to 60 in BayesNetGraph.tsx to prevent glow filter clipping at viewBox edges.

Wire up assumption change detection in BayesNetGraph: add useEffect watching assumptions from useAssumptions(), detect added/removed assumptions, identify evidence node, call triggerAnimation from the hook. Cancel in-progress animation on new change.

Integrate animation state into BayesNetGraph rendering: conditionally apply glow filter/CSS classes to NodeWithDetail elements based on animatingNodes set, add animated stroke-dashoffset or traveling circle along edge <line> elements based on animatingEdges set, add edge wave visual using SVG <circle> with <animateMotion> or CSS animation along edge paths.

Add probability bar coordination: optionally enhance ProbabilityBar transition timing to sync with wave arrival (add brief color flash CSS class that auto-removes). Ensure existing 0.3s ease transition on width remains, add transient highlight class triggered by parent when animation wave arrives at that node.

decomposition_assessment.decision_reason: Total estimated lines (262) is well under the 500-line threshold. Zero high-risk steps. While steps 1-3 are parallelizable, they all modify the same component file (BayesNetGraph.tsx) or closely coupled files, making parallel child spawns impractical. The animation logic is inherently sequential (hook → detection → rendering → coordination), and all changes are concentrated in 2-3 files. A single implementation pass with unified animation state is the most effective approach.
